_comment_project: Arcado Springs landing page. Plain static files (index.html, styles.css, main.js, /screenshots, /unity-build). No framework, no build step.
_comment_brotli: OPTIONAL Brotli support: if you export the Unity WebGL player with Compression Format = Brotli and NO decompression fallback, the files are named Build.wasm.br / Build.data.br / Build.framework.js.br. Vercel will NOT re-compress or strip them, so you must declare both the real Content-Type and Content-Encoding: br yourself. Add these objects to the 'headers' array below when you ship a Brotli build: { source: '/(.*)\\.wasm\\.br', headers: [ { key: Content-Type, value: application/wasm }, { key: Content-Encoding, value: br } ] }, { source: '/(.*)\\.data\\.br', headers: [ { key: Content-Type, value: application/octet-stream }, { key: Content-Encoding, value: br } ] }, { source: '/(.*)\\.framework\\.js\\.br', headers: [ { key: Content-Type, value: application/javascript }, { key: Content-Encoding, value: br } ] }
